<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Astro Starter Kit: Basics
2: 
3: ```sh
4: npm create astro@latest -- --template basics
5: ```
6: 
7: > 🧑‍🚀 **Seasoned astronaut?** Delete this file. Have fun!
8: 
9: ## 🚀 Project Structure
10: 
11: Inside of your Astro project, you'll see the following folders and files:
12: 
13: ```text
14: /
15: ├── public/
16: │   └── favicon.svg
17: ├── src
18: │   ├── assets
19: │   │   └── astro.svg
20: │   ├── components
21: │   │   └── Welcome.astro
22: │   ├── layouts
23: │   │   └── Layout.astro
24: │   └── pages
25: │       └── index.astro
26: └── package.json
27: ```
28: 
29: To learn more about the folder structure of an Astro project, refer to [our guide on project structure](https://docs.astro.build/en/basics/project-structure/).
30: 
31: ## 🧞 Commands
32: 
33: All commands are run from the root of the project, from a terminal:
34: 
35: | Command                   | Action                                           |
36: | :------------------------ | :----------------------------------------------- |
37: | `npm install`             | Installs dependencies                            |
38: | `npm run dev`             | Starts local dev server at `localhost:4321`      |
39: | `npm run build`           | Build your production site to `./dist/`          |
40: | `npm run preview`         | Preview your build locally, before deploying     |
41: | `npm run astro ...`       | Run CLI commands like `astro add`, `astro check` |
42: | `npm run astro -- --help` | Get help using the Astro CLI                     |
43: 
44: ## 👀 Want to learn more?
45: 
46: Feel free to check [our documentation](https://docs.astro.build) or jump into our [Discord server](https://astro.build/chat).

(End of file - total 46 lines)
</content>

C:\Users\pavel\projects\lukaspitrik.cz\AGENTS.md file 1: # AGENTS.md - lukaspitrik.cz 2: 3: This file contains guidelines for AI agents working in this repository. 4: 5: ## Project Overview 6: 7: - **Type**: Astro 6 website (lukaspitrik.cz) 8: - **Location**: `dimensional-disk/` 9: - **Framework**: Astro 6.0.5 10: - **Node**: >= 22.12.0 11: - **TypeScript**: Strict mode (`astro/tsconfigs/strict`) 12: 13: ## Build Commands 14: 15: All commands run from the `dimensional-disk/` directory: 16: 17: ```bash 18: # Development 19: npm run dev # Start dev server at localhost:4321 20: 21: # Production 22: npm run build # Build to ./dist/ 23: npm run preview # Preview production build locally 24: 25: # Astro CLI 26: npm run astro ... # Run any Astro command 27: npm run astro check # Type-check the project 28: npm run astro add # Add integrations 29: ``` 30: 31: ### Testing 32: 33: No test framework is currently configured. If adding tests in the future: 34: 35: ```bash 36: # Single test file (if Vitest added) 37: npx vitest run src/components/Button.test.ts 38: 39: # Watch mode 40: npx vitest src/ 41: ``` 42: 43: ## Code Style 44: 45: ### File Organization 46: 47: ``` 48: src/ 49: ├── components/ # Reusable .astro components 50: ├── layouts/ # Page layouts 51: ├── pages/ # Routes (file-based routing) 52: ├── assets/ # Images, SVGs, fonts 53: └── styles/ # Global styles (if added) 54: ``` 55: 56: ### Astro Components (.astro) 57: 58: Use the standard Astro component structure: 59: 60: ```astro 61: --- 62: // Frontmatter: imports, props, logic 63: import Layout from '../layouts/Layout.astro'; 64: import Card from './Card.astro'; 65: 66: interface Props { 67: title: string; 68: } 69: 70: const { title } = Astro.props; 71: --- 72: 73: 74:
75:

{title}

76: 77:
78: 79: 80: 85: ``` 86: 87: ### TypeScript 88: 89: - Always define explicit types for component props 90: - Use `interface` for object types, `type` for unions/primitives 91: - No `any` types — strict mode enforced 92: 93: ```typescript 94: // Good 95: interface Props { 96: title: string; 97: count?: number; 98: status: 'active' | 'inactive'; 99: } 100: 101: // Avoid 102: const props: any = {}; 103: ``` 104: 105: ### Imports 106: 107: - Use relative paths for local imports 108: - Group imports: external → internal → relative 109: - Always import images/assets using `src` property when needed 110: 111: ```typescript 112: import Layout from '../layouts/Layout.astro'; 113: import Button from './Button.astro'; 114: import logo from '../assets/logo.svg'; 115: ``` 116: 117: ### CSS 118: 119: - Use scoped ` 138: ``` 139: 140: ### Naming Conventions 141: 142: | Element | Convention | Example | 143: |---------|-----------|---------| 144: | Components | PascalCase | `UserProfile.astro` | 145: | Props | camelCase | `isActive`, `userId` | 146: | CSS classes | kebab-case | `.hero-section` | 147: | Constants | SCREAMING_SNAKE | `MAX_ITEMS` | 148: | Variables | camelCase | `itemCount` | 149: 150: ### Error Handling 151: 152: - Use try/catch for async operations 153: - Provide fallback values where sensible 154: - Never leave empty catch blocks 155: 156: ```typescript 157: try { 158: const data = await fetchData(); 159: } catch (error) { 160: console.error('Failed to fetch:', error); 161: return null; // or fallback value 162: } 163: ``` 164: 165: ## Git Workflow 166: 167: - Commit messages: descriptive, imperative mood 168: - Keep commits focused (one feature/fix per commit) 169: - Run `npm run build` before committing to verify no build errors 170: 171: ## VS Code Setup 172: 173: Recommended extensions (already in `.vscode/extensions.json`): 174: 175: - `astro-build.astro-vscode` — Astro language support 176: 177: ## Common Tasks 178: 179: ### Add a new page 180: 181: 1. Create file in `src/pages/` (e.g., `about.astro`) 182: 2. Import a layout: `import Layout from '../layouts/Layout.astro';` 183: 3. Wrap content: `

About

` 184: 185: ### Add a component 186: 187: 1. Create in `src/components/` (e.g., `Header.astro`) 188: 2. Import where needed 189: 3. Props defined in frontmatter with interface 190: 191: ### Add dependencies 192: 193: ```bash 194: cd dimensional-disk 195: npm install 196: ``` 197: 198: ### Run Astro CLI commands 199: 200: ```bash 201: cd dimensional-disk 202: npm run astro add tailwind # Add Tailwind 203: npm run astro add mdx # Add MDX support 204: npm run astro sync # Regenerate types 205: ``` 206: 207: ## Performance Notes 208: 209: - Use `loading="lazy"` for below-fold images 210: - Use `fetchpriority="high"` for hero images 211: - Prefer SVG over raster images where possible 212: - Use Astro's built-in image optimization when adding images (End of file - total 212 lines)

<content>1: import { render } from '@astrojs/testing';
2: import Hero from '../components/Hero.astro';
3: 
4: test('Hero renders with image and title', async () => {
